description: World-class life coaching frameworks from the best coaches, psychologists, and thought leaders

brene_brown.source: Brené Brown, PhD, LMSW - University of Houston
brene_brown.credentials: Research professor, 5 NYT bestsellers, 50M+ TED views
brene_brown.core_research: 20 years studying courage, vulnerability, shame, and empathy

vulnerability_as_strength.insight: Vulnerability is not weakness. It's our most accurate measure of courage.
vulnerability_as_strength.coaching_application: When someone shares something hard, honor the courage it took
phrases: That took real courage to say out loud.
Vulnerability isn't weakness. It's actually the birthplace of connection.
The willingness to show up when you can't control the outcome? That's brave.

shame_resilience.insight: Shame needs secrecy, silence, and judgment to grow. It cannot survive empathy.
four_elements: Practice critical awareness (reality check the messages)
Speak shame - name it to tame it
coaching_phrases: Shame tells us we ARE the mistake. Guilt tells us we MADE a mistake. There's a difference.
Who else knows about this? Shame grows in isolation.
What would you say to a friend who told you this same thing?

wholehearted_living.definition: Engaging with the world from a place of worthiness
ten_guideposts: Cultivating authenticity (letting go of what people think)
Cultivating resilient spirit (letting go of numbing)
Cultivating gratitude and joy (letting go of scarcity)
Cultivating intuition (letting go of need for certainty)
Cultivating play and rest (letting go of exhaustion)
Cultivating calm and stillness (letting go of anxiety)
Cultivating meaningful work (letting go of self-doubt)
Cultivating laughter, song, dance (letting go of being cool)
wholehearted_living.coaching_question: What would you need to let go of to live more wholeheartedly?

the_arena.source: Theodore Roosevelt's 'Man in the Arena' speech
the_arena.insight: It's not the critic who counts. If you're not in the arena also getting your ass kicked, I'm not interested in your feedback.
the_arena.coaching_application: Help them distinguish between feedback from people who matter vs. cheap-seat critics
the_arena.phrase: Who's in your arena? Whose opinions actually matter to you?

tony_robbins.credentials: Coached world leaders, elite athletes, Fortune 500 CEOs for 45+ years

six_human_needs.insight: All human behavior is driven by the need to meet these six needs

needs.coaching_question: What do you need to feel safe right now?
Where do you need more adventure in your life?
In what ways do you want to matter?
Who do you feel truly connected to?
Where are you growing right now?
How do you want to contribute to others?

six_human_needs.coaching_application: Most problems come from meeting needs in destructive ways. Find better vehicles for the same needs.

state_management.insight: You can change your emotional state in seconds through physiology, focus, and language

triad.physiology: Change your body, change your state. Stand tall, breathe deep, move.
triad.focus: Where focus goes, energy flows. What you focus on, you feel.
triad.language: The words you use shape your experience. 'I have to' vs 'I get to'.

coaching_phrases: Take a deep breath with me. Good. Now, what shifts?
What are you focusing on right now? What COULD you focus on instead?
Try saying that again, but change 'I can't' to 'I haven't yet'.

the_quality_of_your_life.insight: The quality of your life equals the quality of your questions
empowering_questions: What am I willing to do to make it the way I want?
What am I willing to stop doing?
How can I enjoy the process?

esther_perel.credentials: Belgian psychotherapist, NYT bestselling author, TED talks 30M+ views

erotic_vs_domestic.insight: We want both security and adventure in relationships. These needs often conflict.
erotic_vs_domestic.coaching_application: Help people understand they need BOTH stability AND mystery
erotic_vs_domestic.phrase: Desire needs space. Sometimes the most intimate thing is to give each other room to miss each other.

relational_ambivalence.insight: Modern relationships carry unprecedented expectations - we want our partner to be best friend, co-parent, intellectual equal, AND passionate lover
relational_ambivalence.coaching_application: Normalize that this is hard. No one person can be everything.
relational_ambivalence.phrase: You're asking one person to provide what an entire village used to give. That's a lot of pressure on one relationship.

the_quality_of_relationships.insight: The quality of our relationships determines the quality of our lives
coaching_phrases: How do you show up in this relationship? Is that who you want to be?
What story are you telling yourself about what they meant by that?
Have you asked them? Or are you mind-reading?

mel_robbins.credentials: CNN commentator, TEDx speaker, author of The 5 Second Rule

five_second_rule.insight: If you have an instinct to act on a goal, you must physically move within 5 seconds or your brain will kill it
five_second_rule.science: Based on metacognition - counting backwards interrupts habitual thought patterns
five_second_rule.application: 5-4-3-2-1-GO. Interrupt hesitation before it becomes inaction.
coaching_phrases: What's the tiniest action you could take right now? Like, in the next 5 seconds?
I know you're thinking about it. What if you just... started? 5-4-3-2-1.
Motivation follows action, not the other way around.

high_five_habit.insight: The relationship with yourself sets the tone for every other relationship
high_five_habit.practice: High five yourself in the mirror every morning
high_five_habit.coaching_application: How you talk to yourself matters more than any external voice

simon_sinek.credentials: Author of Start With Why, TED talk 60M+ views

start_with_why.insight: People don't buy WHAT you do, they buy WHY you do it
start_with_why.coaching_application: Help people find their why - their purpose
questions: Why does this matter to you? No, really - WHY?
What would be lost if you stopped doing this?
What do you believe that others don't?

infinite_mindset.insight: Life is not a finite game to win - it's an infinite game to keep playing

finite_vs_infinite.finite: Known players, fixed rules, agreed objective
finite_vs_infinite.infinite: Known and unknown players, changeable rules, objective is to keep playing

coaching_phrases: You're playing to win. But what if the goal was just to keep playing?
Success isn't a destination. It's a direction.
What would change if you stopped comparing yourself to others and started comparing yourself to who you were yesterday?

bj_fogg.source: BJ Fogg, PhD - Stanford Behavior Design Lab
bj_fogg.credentials: Stanford professor, creator of Tiny Habits method

tiny_habits_framework.insight: Change doesn't require willpower. It requires making things tiny and easy.

key_principles.example: Don't 'exercise' - do 2 push-ups after you pee
key_principles.coaching_application: What's the tiniest version of this habit?

key_principles.example: After I [existing habit], I will [tiny new behavior]
key_principles.coaching_application: What do you already do every day that this could attach to?

key_principles.example: Fist pump, say 'good job!', feel pride
key_principles.coaching_application: Celebration wires in the habit. What's your celebration?

coaching_phrases: What's the tiniest version of this you could do?
What's already automatic in your day that this could follow?
How will you celebrate? Seriously - that's the secret ingredient.

acceptance_and_commitment_therapy.core_insight: Psychological flexibility - being present, opening up, and doing what matters

six_processes.description: Allowing thoughts and feelings without struggling against them
six_processes.coaching_phrase: What if you didn't have to fix this feeling? What if you could just... let it be there?

six_processes.description: Seeing thoughts as just thoughts, not commands
six_processes.coaching_phrase: Notice you're having the thought 'I'm not good enough.' That's different from being not good enough.
Where are you right now? Not where your mind is - where are YOU?

six_processes.description: The observing self that is aware of experiences
six_processes.coaching_phrase: There's the part of you having these feelings. And there's the part of you noticing you're having them. Which one is the real you?
Deep down, what do you want to stand for? What kind of person do you want to be?
Knowing what matters to you - what's one small step you could take today?

motivational_interviewing.core_insight: People are more likely to change when they articulate their own reasons for it

spirit.evocation: Drawing out their wisdom, not inserting yours

oars.example: You've shown real strength in dealing with this
It sounds like part of you wants change, and part of you is scared

change_talk.insight: Listen for and amplify their own arguments for change
change_talk.coaching_application: When they say 'I should probably...' reflect it back: 'You're thinking about making a change'

positive_psychology.source: Martin Seligman, PhD - Father of Positive Psychology

P.description: Joy, gratitude, serenity, interest, hope, pride, amusement, inspiration, awe, love
P.coaching_question: What brought you joy this week? Even small things.

E.description: Flow states - being absorbed in activities
E.coaching_question: When do you lose track of time? What are you doing?

R.coaching_question: Who lights you up? Who drains you?

M.description: Belonging to and serving something greater
M.coaching_question: What gives your life meaning beyond yourself?

A.coaching_question: What are you proud of? What do you want to master?

character_strengths.insight: Everyone has signature strengths - using them more leads to flourishing
character_strengths.coaching_application: Help identify and deploy strengths, not just fix weaknesses

self_determination_theory.core_insight: Intrinsic motivation requires three basic psychological needs

three_needs.description: Feeling in control of one's own behavior and goals
three_needs.coaching_question: Is this YOUR goal, or someone else's goal for you?
Where do you feel capable and effective?
Who do you belong to? Who belongs to you?

self_determination_theory.coaching_application: When motivation is lacking, check which need isn't being met

competencies.name: Demonstrates Ethical Practice
competencies.application: Maintain boundaries, confidentiality, act with integrity

competencies.name: Establishes and Maintains Agreements
competencies.application: Clear on what we're working on and how

competencies.name: Cultivates Trust and Safety
competencies.application: Create environment where authentic sharing happens

competencies.name: Maintains Presence
competencies.application: Fully focused, responsive to the whole person

competencies.name: Listens Actively
competencies.application: Hear what's said and unsaid, notice patterns

competencies.name: Evokes Awareness
competencies.application: Facilitate insight and learning through questions

competencies.name: Facilitates Client Growth
competencies.application: Partner to transform learning into action

steps.name: Goal
questions: How will you know you've achieved it?

from_the_masters.rule: Empathy is feeling WITH someone. Sympathy is feeling FOR them. Choose empathy.
from_the_masters.phrase: I don't know what to say, but I'm really glad you told me.
What are you focusing on? Because that's what's going to grow.

from_the_masters.rule: The quality of your relationships determines the quality of your life.
from_the_masters.phrase: How you are with others is how you are with yourself.

from_the_masters.rule: Start with why. People don't buy what you do, they buy why you do it.
from_the_masters.phrase: Before we talk about how, let's talk about why this matters to you.

from_the_masters.rule: Tiny is mighty. Shrink the change to make it stick.
from_the_masters.phrase: What's the smallest version of this that still counts?

from_the_masters.rule: Those who have a 'why' to live can bear with almost any 'how'.
from_the_masters.phrase: What gives your suffering meaning? That's where strength lives.

from_the_masters.rule: The curious paradox is that when I accept myself just as I am, then I can change.
from_the_masters.phrase: What if nothing about you needed to be fixed? What would you do then?

from_the_masters.rule: People will forget what you said, forget what you did, but never forget how you made them feel.
from_the_masters.phrase: I want you to feel heard. That matters more than any advice I could give.

someone_feeling_stuck.key_question: What's the smallest action that would create momentum?

someone_in_shame.key_question: What would you say to a friend in this exact situation?

someone_lost_meaning.key_question: What would you regret NOT doing if you had one year left?

someone_grieving.key_question: What do you need right now - to feel the loss, or take a break from it?

someone_avoiding_change.key_question: What would have to be true for this change to feel worth it?

someone_building_habits.key_question: What existing habit could this attach to?

someone_in_relationship_crisis.key_question: What are you protecting? What are you longing for?
